item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite enjoyed reading the diplomatic negotiations between the Foundation and the GOI. I'd love to see more of this type of reading. +1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The implication is that the used Occult Shit™ to steal the scientists appearance, which also necessitated killing him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Came from SCPD as soon as I heard!

I like the anomaly, I like the narrative, but the narrative seems fairly detached from the anomaly. The anomaly doesn't directly push the narrative forward until the end and it seems to be treated more like some random asset that got jumbled up in this mess most of the time. If, say, 4776 was replaced by a stack of papers that made things more likely to happen or an arcade machine that could manifest stuff, the article would still be functional.

No-vote, for the moment at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hell yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only question I have is why? Attacking the defector I get, but the scientist?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rounderhouse strikes again.
+1 because holy shit this is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That memo at the end was priceless. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Darn, right.
for that instance I completely disregarded the canons.
Thanks for the clarification.

Also, im suprised I didn't add this to my initial post, but great writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, you're wrong, so I guess I'm gonna have to correct you.

The Tiebreaker is only one possible interpretation of O5-13. Infinite realities, infinite canons. In this one, he's not a tiebreaker or anything of the sort. Just another Overseer. Not every interpretation of a certain characters applies to every work they're features in. Such is the way of No Canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please correct me if im wrong, but O5-13 never actually votes.
I believed him to be a tie breaker, only asked to cast a vote when the odds are even.
As a final decisive vote for The Counsil.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to have to no-vote on this one. I like the idea of a Pentagram project being contained by the Foundation, but I'm not a fan of how the character interaction turned out. It felt less of "US Government trying to pull a fast one on the Foundation, only to get caught and hand over control of their project" and more like two individuals sniping at one another1.

+ Show Extra Crit
- Hide Extra Crit

I also wasn't a fan of the payoff. Again, if it was intended to come across as the Foundation threatening Rob Bishop in particular, it was well done, but if the intention was that the Foundation was threatening the US Gov as a whole, I rather liked how it was done in The Woodvale Incident. (TL;DR, Foundation showed what would happen if they stopped containing objects on US soil, which would be a much more impressive threat than revealing their control of the ARES FORGE weapon)

Footnotes
1. If that was the intent, then bravo, you pulled it off exceptionally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Similar, but definitely not the same.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The BOYtm does it again

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: εїз εїз εїз o7 εїз εїз εїз

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry Zyn. o7

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is good, and I hate to be a downer, but I’m 99% sure that exact image is used in SCP-4812

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’ve been reading this article and Goldeneye’s plot and I don’t really see any connection.

Goldeneye is about a weapon that is harmless beyond causing disruption in computers. It is controlled by a rogue person.

This is about a magical satellite that blows stuff up when activated by runes. The story is all at the diplomatic level, institution vs institution.

Literally the only thing they have in common is “satellite”.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean, technically that's what the critique phase is generally for. That said, similarity is fine, and people can like two things that are similar.

rattles, general note, you're coming off as a bit heated here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah yes, because every author on the wiki is aware of every single fictional work ever and knows not to make anything remotely similar to anything created before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is a copy of goldeneye >:( -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ayyy bois it’s the reigenweapon
Nice stuff rounder! Nothing quite like a good ol' political dick measuring contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Christ I've been wondering when this would get posted! Glad to see it up and doing well!

Still kicking myself for not coming up with it first honestly

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the subject matter, but the emails and dialogue read as overly melodramatic to me. Bureaucrats snipe at (and preach to) each other plenty, but a lot of this reads as starting fights that aren't really necessary for no good reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's about damn time we treated the US government like we treat nearly every non-western government on this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am so glad this finally made it out. This was one of my favorite concepts for an article and the execution blends real world and scp universe history quite well.
+1 all the way

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has been in the works for a while now, hope you enjoy.

Thanks to my critters: A Random Day, Jekeled, GeckoGuy2704, Elogee Fishtruck, TheMightyMcB, Tufto, Uncle Nicolini, UraniumEmpire, aismallard, JackalRelated, and Rattles. If I forgot you, please let me know!

A special thanks to :

Geckoguy2704 for pushing me to complete this when I had all but given up on the idea.

A Random Day for inventing PENTAGRAM, the GoI this article was built in mind with.

Image was taken by the United States Department of Defense, and is public domain: https://www.flickr.com/photos/39955793@N07/31655386914/in/album-72157679530010785/

PENTAGRAM logo created by JackalRelated and licensed under CC BY SA 3.0.
